uses: Conanthus havardii, also known as Havard's penstemon, has been traditionally used by Native American tribes for its medicinal properties. Its roots and leaves have been utilized to treat various ailments, including fever and sore throats. The plant's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties make it a potential remedy for skin irritations and wounds. In some cases, it has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for medicinal use.
